arguments.query: Attio CRM developer API key access
Attio developer account request access how to get API credentials
Attio REST API reference documentation endpoints
Attio free plan API access available on all plans
Attio API developer workspace request manual approval

researched_category.notes: Attio is a modern, data-driven CRM platform for managing contacts, deals, pipelines, and revenue workflows.

one_liner.value: A modern, flexible CRM platform for managing contacts, deals, pipelines, records, and automating revenue workflows.

methods.notes: Supports OAuth 2.0 (recommended for multi-workspace apps), API key/access token (recommended for single-workspace agents), and HTTP Basic (not recommended). The API key is used as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

access.notes: API access is available on all plans including Free. Anyone can sign up for a free workspace and generate API keys as a workspace admin. While there is a manual 'developer workspace' option for App SDK development (contact support), the REST API keys can be self-served from any workspace.

summary.value: Attio has a documented public REST API (v2) with OpenAPI specification covering full CRUD on objects, attributes, records (people, companies, deals), lists, entries, tasks, notes, threads, comments, workspace members, webhooks, call recordings, and users. Rate limits: 100 reads/sec, 25 writes/sec. Also has an App SDK for building embedded apps, and a SQL interface.

agent_interface.notes: Attio has an official hosted MCP server at mcp.attio.com/mcp with OAuth authentication. Also has a community MCP server (kesslerio/attio-mcp-server on npm). Composio offers an Attio toolkit. The App SDK allows building embedded apps with React/TypeScript.

rationale.value: Attio has a well-documented, broad REST API that is accessible on the free plan with self-serve API key generation. It also provides an official hosted MCP server (mcp.attio.com/mcp), a community MCP server, and is available via Composio's toolkit. A developer can sign up for free, create API credentials without any approval gate, and build an agent toolkit today.

s1.quote: Full documentation index listing all REST API endpoints including objects, attributes, companies, people, deals, lists, entries, tasks, notes, threads, comments, workspace members, webhooks, call recordings.

s2.quote: In order to make requests to the Attio REST API, you need to generate an access token. There are two ways: 1. By implementing an OAuth 2.0 flow 2. By generating an API key for your workspace

s3.quote: Both OAuth access tokens and single-workspace access token are used in the same way. Pass the value of the token in the `Authorization` header of your requests like so. Authorization: Bearer <access_token>

s4.quote: We also support HTTP Basic Authentication, where the username is the token and the password is left blank. However, we recommend using Bearer authentication where possible.

s5.quote: API and webhook access — Free, Plus, Pro, Enterprise

s6.quote: Available on all plans. Admins can create and manage API keys.

s7.quote: Alternatively, you could also sign up for a free trial and use that workspace as a dev sandbox (though you'll need to seed it yourself).

s8.quote: Attio exposes a public OpenAPI specification for the Attio REST API.

s10.quote: Attio API rate limits allow 100 read requests per second and 25 write requests per second, with HTTP 429 returned when limits are exceeded.

s11.quote: Attio MCP is Attio's hosted MCP server that gives AI tools secure access to your Attio workspace. URL: https://mcp.attio.com/mcp

s12.quote: A comprehensive Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for Attio, providing complete CRM surface coverage.

s13.quote: Our new MCP server let's you search, query, and act on your Attio data, from any MCP client.

s14.quote: Managed auth, hosted MCP servers, and every Attio tool your agent needs. Free to start.

limitations: The 'developer workspace' that requires manual request via live chat/email is specifically for App SDK developers building published apps; for REST API access, a regular free workspace is sufficient.
Admin role required to generate API keys within a workspace, but workspace creator is admin by default.
